task_completion.description: Verify the Python script is created at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, IDE, or terminal showing a file named 'snells_law.py' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the script contains the logic to calculate the angle of refraction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Python code calculating the refraction angle using math functions (e.g., math.asin, math.sin, math.radians) with the values 1.00 (Air), 1.52 (Crown Glass), and an incident angle of 45.

task_completion.description: Verify the script is set to save the output as an SVG file at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Python code that opens or writes to a file named '/home/ga/Documents/refraction.svg'.

task_completion.description: Verify the SVG includes the required graphical elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SVG generation code (e.g., <line>, <path> tags) in the script, or a rendered SVG image, showing the boundary interface, normal line, incident ray, and refracted ray.

task_completion.description: Verify the SVG includes the required text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SVG text elements (<text> tags) in the script, or a rendered SVG image, containing the words 'Air', 'Glass', '45', and the calculated refracted angle (approximately '27.7').

integrity.description: Ensure the refraction angle is actually calculated programmatically.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the Python script to ensure the angle ~27.7 is derived from a mathematical calculation (e.g., using Snell's Law formula) and not just hardcoded as a string or float.

integrity.description: Ensure the SVG is generated by the Python script and not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent writes Python code to generate and save the SVG file, rather than opening a text editor to manually write the SVG XML code.

integrity.description: Ensure the generated SVG code has a valid structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the Python script writes proper SVG boilerplate (e.g., <svg ...>, xmlns attributes) and closes the tags properly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Python script must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/snells_law.py and the SVG at /home/ga/Documents/refraction.svg. The calculated refraction angle should be approximately 27.7 degrees (matching regex 27\.7\d*). The SVG must include text labels for 'Air', 'Glass', '45', and the calculated angle (~27.7).
